question: Which lab will have the highest score on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index on August 31, 2026?

factors.description: Anthropic holds both #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60/64.9) and #2 (Opus 4.8, ~56), ~5 pts ahead; freshest top model (launched June 9, 2026). Any challenger must exceed ~60 to overtake.
OpenAI is closest rival (~55, GPT-5.5/5.6) and historically leapfrogs — briefly held #1 in April 2026; likely to ship a new frontier model within 2 months.
AA Index reorders frequently; over 2 months the leader is often whoever ships newest — adds uncertainty against the status-quo leader.
Z AI (GLM-5.2, ~52) is ~8 pts behind #1; fast-moving open lab but a large gap to close to reach outright #1 in 2 months.
'Other' includes Google DeepMind (~50, could ship a higher Gemini), xAI, DeepSeek, Alibaba — collectively a real leapfrog chance.

summary: Anthropic is the clear status-quo leader on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index as of early July 2026, holding both #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60) and #2 (Opus 4.8, ~56), roughly 5 points ahead of the nearest rival. Over the ~2-month horizon to Aug 31, the main risk to Anthropic is the index's frequent reordering — OpenAI briefly led in April 2026 before Fable 5 retook #1 in June — meaning whoever ships the newest frontier model often takes the top spot. OpenAI (~55) is the strongest challenger; Google (in 'Other', ~50) is a credible dark horse; Z AI (GLM-5.2, ~52) is ~8 points back and unlikely to be outright #1 in two months. Any challenger must ship a model scoring above ~60 within the window. I keep meaningful probability across all four options given release-timing uncertainty, while favoring Anthropic.
reasoning:
Forecast submitted.

**Which lab will lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index on Aug 31, 2026?**

- **Anthropic: 50%** — Current clear leader, holding both #1 (Claude Fable 5, ~60) and #2 (Opus 4.8), ~5 points ahead. Freshest top model. Any rival must ship a model scoring above ~60 to overtake.
- **OpenAI: 28%** — Strongest challenger (~55), historically leapfrogs (briefly led in April 2026), likely to ship a new frontier model within the window.
- **Other: 15%** — Google DeepMind (~50) is a credible dark horse; also xAI, DeepSeek, Alibaba.
- **Z AI: 7%** — GLM-5.2 (~52) is credible but ~8 points back, unlikely to reach outright #1 in two months.

The key uncertainty is release timing: over a 2-month horizon on a frequently-reordering leaderboard, the top spot often goes to whoever ships the newest frontier model — which is why meaningful probability stays on the challengers despite Anthropic's current lead.
